problem.name: tsp_benchmark
problem.description: Euclidean TSP on the TSPLIB95 benchmark (known optima). The solver is run on several dev instances; score = mean gap %% above the known optimum (lower is better, 0 = optimal on every instance). Distances use the TSPLIB metric: each edge rounded to the nearest integer. The winning solver is re-evaluated on held-out instances at the end.
problem.stats: TSPLIB95 dev set: berlin52 (52 cities), st70 (70 cities), eil76 (76 cities), kroA100 (100 cities), rd100 (100 cities). Optima are known; score is the mean gap %% above optimum across all dev instances (TSPLIB rounded-integer metric). 6 held-out instances are kept hidden for final verification.

hypotheses.name: Or-opt + 2-opt Hybrid
hypotheses.strategy: Local Search with Or-opt (1,2,3-city chain relocation) combined with 2-opt
hypotheses.hypothesis: Augmenting 2-opt with Or-opt moves (relocating chains of 1, 2, and 3 consecutive cities to better positions) will reduce the mean gap below 5.40% because Or-opt explores a richer neighborhood than 2-opt alone, escaping local optima that 2-opt cannot, while remaining fast enough for 52–100 city instances within 10 seconds.
hypotheses.risk: Or-opt and 2-opt may converge to the same local optima on these instances if the nearest-neighbor initialization is poor; without restarts, the improvement may be insufficient.

hypotheses.name: Iterated Local Search with 3-opt
hypotheses.strategy: Iterated Local Search (ILS) using 3-opt as the local search and double-bridge perturbation for escaping local optima
hypotheses.hypothesis: Applying 3-opt local search within an ILS framework (using double-bridge moves as perturbation) will achieve a mean gap below 5.40% because 3-opt eliminates crossing edges that 2-opt misses, and double-bridge perturbation provides non-sequential escapes from local optima, which is known to be highly effective on TSPLIB instances of this size range.
hypotheses.risk: 3-opt has O(n^3) complexity per pass; for 100-city instances under time pressure, the number of ILS iterations may be too few to find significantly better solutions.

hypotheses.name: LKH-style Lin-Kernighan with Candidate Lists
hypotheses.strategy: Lin-Kernighan moves with restricted candidate lists (5 nearest neighbors per city) for efficient sequential search
hypotheses.hypothesis: Implementing Lin-Kernighan style variable-depth moves restricted to 5-nearest-neighbor candidate lists will achieve a mean gap below 5.40% because LK moves generalize 2-opt and 3-opt in a single framework and candidate list restriction makes each move O(k*n) rather than O(n^2), enabling many restarts within the time budget on instances up to 100 cities.
hypotheses.risk: A pure-Python LK implementation may be too slow to execute enough moves within 10 seconds, especially if candidate list construction overhead is not carefully managed.

hypotheses.name: Multi-restart Greedy + 2-opt with Best-of-N Selection
hypotheses.strategy: Multiple random restarts using greedy (cheapest insertion or random nearest-neighbor) initialization followed by 2-opt, keeping the best tour found
hypotheses.hypothesis: Running 20–50 independent restarts of greedy initialization + 2-opt and selecting the best solution will achieve a mean gap below 5.40% because the baseline uses only a single nearest-neighbor start; diverse initializations avoid the worst local optima and statistically sample better 2-opt basins, with each restart completing in well under 1 second for ≤100 cities.
hypotheses.risk: All restarts may converge to similar quality local optima if the 2-opt basin structure is highly clustered, meaning more restarts yield diminishing returns rather than finding genuinely better solutions.

import math
import random
import time

def solve(cities: list[list[float]]) -> list[int]:
    n = len(cities)
    if n <= 3:
        return list(range(n))
    
    # Precompute distance matrix with TSPLIB rounding
    dist = [[0] * n for _ in range(n)]
    for i in range(n):
        xi, yi = cities[i]
        for j in range(n):
            xj, yj = cities[j]
            dx = xi - xj
            dy = yi - yj
            dist[i][j] = int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)
    
    def tour_length(tour):
        return sum(dist[tour[i]][tour[(i+1) % n]] for i in range(n))
    
    def nearest_neighbor(start):
        visited = [False] * n
        tour = [start]
        visited[start] = True
        for _ in range(n - 1):
            curr = tour[-1]
            best_next = -1
            best_d = float('inf')
            for j in range(n):
                if not visited[j] and dist[curr][j] < best_d:
                    best_d = dist[curr][j]
                    best_next = j
            tour.append(best_next)
            visited[best_next] = True
        return tour
    
    def two_opt(tour):
        improved = True
        best = tour[:]
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for i in range(1, n - 1):
                for j in range(i + 1, n):
                    a, b = best[i-1], best[i]
                    c, d = best[j], best[(j+1) % n]
                    if dist[a][b] + dist[c][d] > dist[a][c] + dist[b][d]:
                        best[i:j+1] = best[i:j+1][::-1]
                        improved = True
        return best
    
    def or_opt(tour, chain_len):
        """Or-opt: try relocating chains of chain_len consecutive cities."""
        improved = True
        best = tour[:]
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for i in range(n):
                # Chain: indices i, i+1, ..., i+chain_len-1 (mod n)
                # Extract chain positions
                chain_indices = [(i + k) % n for k in range(chain_len)]
                prev_i = (i - 1) % n
                next_chain = (i + chain_len) % n

                city_prev = best[prev_i]
                city_chain = [best[ci] for ci in chain_indices]
                city_next = best[next_chain]
                
                # Cost of removing chain from current position
                remove_gain = (dist[city_prev][city_chain[0]] + 
                               dist[city_chain[-1]][city_next] -
                               dist[city_prev][city_next])
                
                # Try inserting chain after each other position

                    # Skip positions that are in or adjacent to chain

                            skip = True
                            break
                    if skip:
                        continue

                            in_chain = True

                    if in_chain:

                    city_j = best[j]
                    city_next_j = best[next_j]
                    
                    # Cost of inserting chain between j and next_j

import math
import time
import random

def solve(cities: list[list[float]]) -> list[int]:
    n = len(cities)
    if n <= 3:
        return list(range(n))
    
    # Precompute rounded integer distances (TSPLIB EUC_2D)
    def dist(i, j):
        dx = cities[i][0] - cities[j][0]
        dy = cities[i][1] - cities[j][1]
        return int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)
    
    # Build distance matrix for small n
    if n <= 150:
        D = [[0]*n for _ in range(n)]
        for i in range(n):
            for j in range(n):
                if i != j:
                    D[i][j] = dist(i, j)
    else:
        D = None
    
    def d(i, j):
        if D is not None:
            return D[i][j]
        return dist(i, j)
    
    # Build candidate lists (nearest neighbors)
    k_candidates = min(15, n-1)
    candidates = []
    for i in range(n):
        dists = [(d(i, j), j) for j in range(n) if j != i]
        dists.sort()
        candidates.append([j for _, j in dists[:k_candidates]])
    
    # Tour length
    def tour_length(tour):
        return sum(d(tour[i], tour[(i+1) % n]) for i in range(n))
    
    # Nearest neighbor construction
    def nearest_neighbor(start):
        visited = [False] * n
        tour = [start]
        visited[start] = True
        for _ in range(n - 1):
            curr = tour[-1]
            best = -1
            best_d = float('inf')
            for j in candidates[curr]:
                if not visited[j]:
                    dd = d(curr, j)
                    if dd < best_d:
                        best_d = dd
                        best = j
            if best == -1:
                for j in range(n):
                    if not visited[j]:
                        dd = d(curr, j)
                        if dd < best_d:
                            best_d = dd
                            best = j
            tour.append(best)
            visited[best] = True
        return tour
    
    # 2-opt with candidate lists and don't-look bits
    def two_opt(tour):
        improved = True
        pos = [0] * n
        for i, c in enumerate(tour):
            pos[c] = i
        dlb = [False] * n
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for idx in range(n):
                t1 = tour[idx]
                if dlb[t1]:
                    continue
                found = False
                t1_next = tour[(idx + 1) % n]
                d_t1_next = d(t1, t1_next)
                for t2 in candidates[t1]:
                    # Check gain potential
                    if d(t1, t2) >= d_t1_next:
                        break
                    pos_t2 = pos[t2]
                    t2_next = tour[(pos_t2 + 1) % n]
                    if t2_next == t1:
                        continue
                    gain = d_t1_next + d(t2, t2_next) - d(t1, t2) - d(t1_next, t2_next)
                    if gain > 0:
                        # Reverse segment between t1_next and t2
                        i1 = (idx + 1) % n
                        i2 = pos_t2
                        # Make sure we reverse the right segment
                        if i1 <= i2:
                            seg = tour[i1:i2+1]
                            seg.reverse()
                            tour[i1:i2+1] = seg
                        else:
                            # Wrap around - reverse the other segment
                            seg = tour[i2+1:idx+1]
                            seg.reverse()
                            tour[i2+1:idx+1] = seg
                        # Update positions
                        for k in range(n):
                            pos[tour[k]] = k
                        improved = True
                        found = True
                        dlb[t1] = False
                        dlb[t2] = False
                        break
                if not found:
                    dlb[t1] = True
        return tour
    
    # Or-opt: move segments of size 1, 2, 3
    def or_opt(tour):
        improved = True
        pos = [0] * n
        for i, c in enumerate(tour):
            pos[c] = i
        
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for seg_len in [1, 2, 3]:
                for i in range(n):
                    # Segment: tour[i], tour[i+1], ..., tour[i+seg_len-1]
                    i1 = i
                    i2 = (i + seg_len - 1) % n
                    prev_i = (i1 - 1) % n
                    next_i2 = (i2 + 1) % n
                    
                    if prev_i == i2 or next_i2 == i1:
                        continue
                    
                    A = tour[prev_i]
                    seg_start = tour[i1]
                    seg_end = tour[i2]
                    B = tour[next_i2]
                    
                    # Cost of removing segment
                    remove_gain = d(A, seg_start) + d(seg_end, B) - d(A, B)
                    
                    # Try inserting after each candidate of seg_start
                    for C in candidates[seg_start]:
                        if C == A or C == seg_end:
                            continue
                        pos_C = pos[C]
                        D_city = tour[(pos_C + 1) % n]
                        if D_city == seg_start:
                            continue
                        # Check C is not in segment
                        in_seg = False
                        for s in range(seg_len):
                            if tour[(i1 + s) % n] == C:
                                in_seg = True
                                break
                        if in_seg:
                            continue
                        
                        insert_cost = d(C, seg_start) + d(seg_end, D_city) - d(C, D_city)
                        gain = remove_gain - insert_cost
                        
                        if gain > 0:
                            # Extract segment
                            if i1 <= i2:
                                seg = tour[i1:i2+1]
                            else:
                                seg = tour[i1:] + tour[:i2+1]
                            
                            # Rebuild tour without segment
                            new_tour = []
                            j = next_i2
                            while len(new_tour) < n - seg_len:
                                new_tour.append(tour[j])
                                j = (j + 1) % n
                            
                            # Find position of C in new_tour and insert after it
                            ins_pos = new_tour.index(C)
                            tour = new_tour[:ins_pos+1] + seg + new_tour[ins_pos+1:]
                            
                            for k in range(n):
                                pos[tour[k]] = k
                            improved = True
                            break
        return tour
    
    # 3-opt move (simplified: try all 3-opt combinations for segments)
    def three_opt_segment(tour):
        """Simple 3-opt using segment reversal patterns"""
        improved = True
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for i in range(n - 2):
                t1 = tour[i]
                t2 = tour[i+1]
                d_t1_t2 = d(t1, t2)
                for j in range(i+2, n - 1):
                    t3 = tour[j]
                    t4 = tour[j+1]
                    d_t3_t4 = d(t3, t4)
                    # Check if worth continuing
                    if d(t1, t3) >= d_t1_t2 + d_t3_t4:
                        pass  # might still find improvement with k
                    for k in range(j+2, n + (1 if i > 0 else 0)):
                        kk = k % n
                        t5 = tour[kk]
                        t6 = tour[(kk+1) % n]
                        if t6 == t1:
                            # Edge t5-t6 is t5-t1, which connects back
                            pass
                        
                        d0 = d_t1_t2 + d_t3_t4 + d(t5, t6)
                        
                        # Try the 8 combinations of 3-opt
                        # Only the non-2-opt ones:
                        # Pattern: reverse both segments
                        d1 = d(t1, t3) + d(t2, t5) + d(t4, t6)
                        if d1 < d0:
                            # Reverse [i+1..j] and [j+1..k]
                            seg1 = tour[i+1:j+1]
                            seg1.reverse()
                            seg2 = tour[j+1:kk+1]
                            seg2.reverse()
                            tour[i+1:j+1] = seg1
                            tour[j+1:kk+1] = seg2
                            improved = True
                            break
                        
                        # Pattern: move segment
                        d2 = d(t1, t4) + d(t5, t2) + d(t3, t6)
                        if d2 < d0:
                            # This is a segment move: put [j+1..k] between i and i+1
                            seg = tour[j+1:kk+1]
                            rest = tour[i+1:j+1]
                            tour[i+1:kk+1] = seg + rest
                            improved = True
                            break
                        
                        d3 = d(t1, t4) + d(t5, t3) + d(t2, t6)
                        if d3 < d0:
                            seg1 = tour[i+1:j+1]
                            seg2 = tour[j+1:kk+1]
                            seg2.reverse()
                            tour[i+1:kk+1] = seg2 + seg1
                            improved = True
                            break
                    
                    if improved:
                        break
                if improved:
                    break
        return tour
    
    # Double-bridge perturbation
    def double_bridge(tour):
        # Split tour into 4 segments and reconnect differently
        positions = sorted(random.sample(range(1, n), 3))
        a, b, c = positions
        seg1 = tour[:a]
        seg2 = tour[a:b]
        seg3 = tour[b:c]
        seg4 = tour[c:]
        # Reconnect: seg1 + seg3 + seg2 + seg4
        return seg1 + seg3 + seg2 + seg4
    
    # Full local search: 2-opt + or-opt
    def local_search(tour):
        tour = two_opt(tour)
        tour = or_opt(tour)
        tour = two_opt(tour)
        return tour
    
    start_time = time.time()
    time_limit = 9.0
    
    # Generate initial solution with multiple NN starts
    best_tour = None
    best_len = float('inf')
    
    for start in range(min(n, 10)):
        t = nearest_neighbor(start)
        t = local_search(t)
        tl = tour_length(t)
        if tl < best_len:
            best_len = tl
            best_tour = t[:]
    
    # ILS loop
    no_improve_count = 0
    current_tour = best_tour[:]
    current_len = best_len
    
    iteration = 0
    while time.time() - start_time < time_limit:
        iteration += 1
        
        # Perturbation
        new_tour = double_bridge(current_tour)
        
        # Local search
        new_tour = local_search(new_tour)
        new_len = tour_length(new_tour)
        
        # Accept if better
        if new_len < current_len:
            current_tour = new_tour[:]
            current_len = new_len
            if new_len < best_len:
                best_len = new_len
                best_tour = new_tour[:]
                no_improve_count = 0
        else:
            no_improve_count += 1
        
        # Restart from best if stuck
        if no_improve_count > 20:
            current_tour = best_tour[:]
            current_len = best_len
            no_improve_count = 0
    
    return best_tour
